title: What Happened in Afghanistan?
summary: What happened in Afghanistan? This was the overriding question following the withdrawal of U.S. troops in the war-ridden country on September 11, 2021, following 20 years of American involvement. In four events during October and early November organized by Harvard Kennedy School Dean Douglas Elmendorf, Kennedy School faculty and senior fellows discussed what went wrong (and right) in Afghanistan, how U.S. actions—including the troop withdrawal—impacted the Afghan people and our international relationships, and what lessons we’ve learned or should have learned.
body: Featured in the Fall 2021 Newsletter » Belfer Center Experts Reflect on U.S. Actions and Impacts What happened in Afghanistan? This was the overriding question following the withdrawal of U.S. troops in the war-ridden country on September 11, 2021, following 20 years of American involvement. In four events during October and early November organized by Harvard Kennedy School Dean Douglas Elmendorf , Kennedy School faculty and senior fellows discussed what went wrong (and right) in Afghanistan, how U.S. actions—including the troop withdrawal—impacted the Afghan people and our international relationships, and what lessons we’ve learned or should have learned. A number of Belfer Center experts took part in these discussions to share their reflections. On October 6, Dean Elmendorf moderated a discussion on Afghanistan in the JFK Jr. Forum with Belfer Center Director and former Secretary of Defense Ash Carter. Also in October, the Dean's Chief of Staff Sarah Wald hosted the first of several panels to examine different aspects of U.S. involvement in Afghanistan. Eric Rosenbach , Meghan O’Sullivan , and Farah Pandith —all of whom served in government during some parts of the 20-year war—offered different perspectives on what happened and lessons learned: No easy consensus emerged among experts with decades of combined national security work since 9/11. In the second panel in the series, panelists examined why Afghanistan fell to the Taliban and suggested next steps to increase stability. The Belfer Center’s Zoe Marks , along with HKS faculty members Ricardo Hausman and Asim Khwaja , responded to the question of what’s next for Afghanistan: In a discussion as varied as the panelists, a few hopeful themes emerged from an evaluation of how Afghanistan might move forward The final panel in the series looked at whether the withdrawal of forces was a wise or unwise policy decision. The three Belfer Center-affiliated experts— Graham Allison , Stephen Walt , and Kathryn Sikkink —all argued that while the pullout was messy, it was the right thing to do: Three senior foreign affairs analysts challenge the common view that U.S. credibility was undermined by military departure from Kabul ### These Afghanistan series articles are courtesy of the Harvard Kennedy School Office of Communications and Public Affairs. The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "What Happened in Afghanistan?" Belfer Center Newsletter. Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: Following the U.S. troop withdrawal from Afghanistan in September 2021, the Harvard Kennedy School organized a series of discussions in October and early November to analyze the event. Experts, including several from the Belfer Center, reflected on what went wrong and right during the 20-year involvement. The panels examined the impacts of U.S. actions on the Afghan people and international relations, the reasons behind the Taliban's takeover, and potential paths forward for stability. While no easy consensus emerged, some experts argued that despite the messy execution, the withdrawal itself was a correct policy decision, challenging the notion that it severely undermined U.S. credibility. The discussions aimed to extract lessons learned from the prolonged conflict.
result.main_content_zh: 2021年9月美军从阿富汗撤军后，哈佛大学肯尼迪学院于同年10月至11月初组织了一系列讨论活动，以分析这一事件。包括贝尔弗中心多位专家在内的参与者，反思了美国20年介入期间的对与错。系列研讨会探讨了美国行动对阿富汗人民及国际关系的影响、塔利班重新掌权的原因，以及阿富汗未来实现稳定的可能路径。尽管专家们并未达成简单共识，但部分专家认为，尽管撤军过程混乱，但撤军本身是正确的政策决定，并反驳了此举严重损害美国信誉的普遍观点。这些讨论旨在从这场旷日持久的冲突中汲取教训。
